Overview: Find the collapse witness and the break point before you discuss more content, and reject any repair recommendation that is not just numeric tuning, not just more content, still keeps the same dominant line, and still lets the old answer survive with the same read, tradeoff, or same consequence.
Default Workflow: Demand a repair recommendation with a structural fix that changes read, tradeoff, or consequence, names what old answer stops working, what new answer becomes correct, which reward loop currently trains the wrong habit, what right habit should replace it, and what reward, information, or cost shift causes that behavior shift before you call the loop fixed.
Quality Checks: Hard fail any solved-state repair that keeps the same dominant line, the same read, the same consequence structure, or the old answer alive inside the same decision landscape before balance values are tuned.
Hard fail any variation pass that names variation but keeps the same dominant line, the same read, the same consequence, or never says what old answer stops working and what new answer is required.

Hard fail any reinforcement pass that names wrong habit to right habit but never states which reward loop trains the wrong behavior, what player behavior must disappear, what replacement behavior must become optimal, or what reward, information, or cost shift causes the behavior shift.
Failure Patterns and Fixes: Reject numeric-only or content-only fixes, write a structural repair recommendation, call out when numeric-only tuning keeps the same dominant line, the same read, and the same consequence structure, and rewrite the fix so the decision landscape changes before balance values are tuned.

Overview: Do not sell the method; state the pressure test and move straight into the audit.
Quality Checks: Keep only checks that reveal collapse, autopilot, or wrong reinforcement.

Hard fail any solved-state repair that keeps the same dominant line, the same read, the same consequence structure, or the old answer alive inside the same decision landscape before balance values are tuned.
Hard fail any variation pass that names variation but keeps the same dominant line, the same read, the same consequence, or never says what old answer stops working and what new answer is required.

Hard fail any reinforcement pass that names wrong habit to right habit but never states which reward loop trains the wrong behavior, what player behavior must disappear, what replacement behavior must become optimal, or what reward, information, or cost shift causes the behavior shift.
Hard fail any stop condition that names a break point without naming the collapse witness the player can actually observe.

Failure Patterns and Fixes: Treat any loop without a collapse witness or break point, or any repair recommendation that still keeps the same read, tradeoff, same consequence, dominant line, or old answer alive, as unshippable until the structural fix is explicit.

Output Format: Keep the deliverable focused on collapse point, solved state, repair move, and the next pressure test.
